https://www.sloanehelicopters.com/_/uplo.title: B1.3 Licensed Engineer
https://www.sloanehelicopters.com/_/uplo.description: Sloane Helicopters has a long and excellent reputation for providing helicopter EMS services and has been providing air ambulance support since 1999. Due to continued expansion, we now have an exciting opportunity for a B1.3 Licensed Engineer to join the Sloane Helicopters team in support of the Essex & Herts Air Ambulance, based at North Weald and Earls Colne. This is a fantastic opportunity to utilise and develop your existing skills to make a real difference in the world and to play a vital part in saving lives. Reporting to the Line Maintenance Supervisor, this is a unique position for an engineer who is happy to work on their own initiative with a great deal of autonomy whilst, at the same time, being part of an experienced and supportive team. The successful candidate will hold a B1.3 licence and, although not essential, ideally with type-ratings for AW169/AW109 aircraft. They will also need the personal discipline to maintain the aircraft in their care to a consistent high standard. Working at Sloane is not just a job. Becoming part of the Sloane team will give you a real sense of belonging and you will be joining at a very exciting point in the company’s continued evolution. We want to nurture and develop our employees to ensure they reach their full potential and to help drive the company forward to greater success. In addition to a competitive salary, you will also be eligible to be enrolled on the new Employee Bonus Scheme which will grow in direct correlation with the company’s success. Due to the mobile nature of this role, you will also be supplied with a company van. Other benefits will include: Contributory Pension Scheme – 5% company contribution, Private Healthcare, Death in Service – 3x salary, Income Protection Scheme. If using your skills and experience to help save lives sounds like the role that you have been waiting for, please forward your CV by email, together with details of your current salary, to Mrs Andrea Peel - apeel@sloanehelicopters.com. Your CALLING Charity Needs B1.3 LICENSED ENGINEERS YOU!

https://nhv-group.jobtoolz.com/en/techni.title: Technical Director
https://nhv-group.jobtoolz.com/en/techni.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth.
This function can be performed from any of the NHV bases. Regular travel to other bases is required.
Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required.
Financial & Performance Management Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards.
People Leadership & Culture Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication.
Safety, Compliance & Governance Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices.
Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions.
Stakeholder Management Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth.
Skills & Experience Extensive senior leadership experience in aviation technical management or a comparable highly regulated industry. Proven experience as (Nominated) Postholder under an AOC and/or as Accountable Manager. In-depth knowledge of aviation maintenance regulations and regulatory frameworks. Strong financial acumen, including P&L ownership and cost control. Demonstrated success in leading transformation, restructuring, and performance optimization initiatives. Experience in an international, multi-base operational environment. Solid understanding of business planning, operational management, and customer contract management. Results-driven, structured, and proactive mindset. Strong analytical capabilities with a KPI-focused approach to performance management. Professional level of English (spoken and written).
What we offer Join the Thrilling World of Aviation Step into an exciting career in an international helicopter services company that combines a youthful spirit with over 25 years of industry expertise. What We Offer When you join our team, you can count on a competitive salary package. This role includes regular travel to our other offices. The home base is not fixed. If you’re a experienced and strategic leader who thrives in a hands-on, fast-paced helicopter environment and takes pride in delivering safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations, we’d love to hear from you!

https://nhv-group.jobtoolz.com/en/experi.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
https://nhv-group.jobtoolz.com/en/experi.description:
NHV Group is a European based helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
Are you an experienced Helicopter Hoist Operator and licensed engineer with a passion for safety and efficiency? Join our team to execute both scheduled and unscheduled Helicopter Hoist Operations (HHO) flights and crew change flights.
The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role.
You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests.
Responsibilities and Main Results Areas
Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO)
Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's.
Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual.
Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment.
Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew.
Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights.
Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s).
Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo.
Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning).
Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise.
Use standardized aviation terminology.
Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues.
Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy.
Line Maintenance
Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions
Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable.
Ensure all safety regulations are followed
Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system.
Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures.
Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous.
Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified.
Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings.
Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks.
Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order.
Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity.
Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs.
Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.
Skills and Experience
Experience
·         Experience as a Helicopter Hoist Operator
·         Minimum 5 years of relevant experience as a licensed engineer.
·         Experience working in a line maintenance environment.
·         Engineering experience in different aviation companies is a plus.
Skills
·         Medical class 2 is required.
·         Languages: English (minimum ICAO Level 4)
·         EASA Part 66 B1.3 license or B2 license.
·         Type rating H175 is preferred
·         ICT literate (Rusada Envision knowledge is a plus)
·         Good communicator:
- Debriefing with the flight crew
- Briefing of passengers
- Handover of work from/to previous/next shift
- Communication with the maintenance lead
·         Able to work independently when performing in a less controlled environment and able to take initiative to improve work and complete tasks within appropriate timeframe.
·         Problem solver / logic thinking.
What we offer
NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore.
We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry.
At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions.
Our expertise spans across Oil and Gas, Offshore Wind, Maritime Services, MRO Services, ATO Pilot Training, and P147 Technician Training. We take pride in working with a skilled and passionate team who ensure everything meets the highest industry standards.
Joining NHV means becoming part of an innovative international aviation company that values motivation, enthusiasm, and dynamism. We prioritize our employees, supporting their well-being and growth with comprehensive, role-specific training. Our commitment to fostering a positive work environment includes organizing corporate events and encouraging innovative and critical thinking.

https://apply.workable.com/billings-flyi.description:
The Pilot-In-Command reports directly to the Chief Pilot/Assistant Chief Pilot and is responsible for the safe and efficient conduct of the flight assignment. The Chief Pilot/Assistant Chief Pilot may delegate certain functions to the PIC to be authorized to conduct business in the absence of the Chief Pilot/Assistant Chief Pilot to meet certain Company and FAA requirements. The PIC will be responsible for all duties and responsibilities required as a Pilot in Command in accordance with the BFS General Operations Manual (GOM).
Qualifications
PIC must be able to maintain thorough understanding of Billings Flying Services policies and procedures and must embody, exhibit, and ensure Billings Flying Services core values in everyday work.
PIC must be highly knowledgeable of FAA regulations, Operations Specifications, Flight Manuals and to the content of all BFS General Operations Manual, Training Manual, Standard Operating Procedures, and Approved/Accepted FAA/USFS Manual.
Minimum of 3500 total time / 2500 PIC and 1500 Turbine. Tandem Rotor time a plus.
Must satisfy the applicable USFS hour requirements.
USFS Aerial Firefighting experience in Helicopters
Maintain a Second-Class Medical.
Type Rated in the CH-47D (BV234) is favorable.
Vertical Reference proficient
NVG qualified
Available for Domestic and International assignments.
Duties and Responsibilities
Safety of Flight
Ability to maintain active Supervisory Responsibilities of field crew and copilots. Issue directives and notices to flight crews as required.
PIC may be requested to assist and perform OJT for flight crewmember training, PIC upgrade training, flight base audits/inspections, and any other aviation duties as directed by the Chief Pilot.
Participate in pilot training when qualified to do so.
Actively participate in ensuring Billings Flying Services Safety Management System (SMS) including reporting hazards and incidents encountered in daily operations; understanding, complying and promoting the Company Safety Policy and other safety duties as assigned.
Reviews flight assignments and obtain briefing information regarding the purpose of the flight, weather, operating procedures, and any special instructions.
Prepares or supervises preparation of flight plan considering such factors as altitude, terrain, weather, range, weight, cruise performance, airport facilities and navigational aids.
Ensures aircraft is preflight, inspected, loaded, equipped, and manned for the flight assignment.
Determine compliance with duty and flight time limitations, properly certificated, adequately rested and in proper dress.
Ensures that all required forms, equipment, and material are on board the aircraft prior to flight.
Records mechanical irregularities and discrepancies and ensures that maintenance personnel are informed of the item recorded.
Verifies before beginning any flight that recorded items have been procedurally complied with, deferred, or corrected by maintenance personnel in accordance with the approved Minimum Equipment List for that aircraft, as appropriate.
CFR 91.3 Responsibility and authority of pilot in command. The pilot in command of an aircraft is directly responsible for, and is the final authority as to, the operation of that aircraft, to include weather decisions.

· Embody BFS core values
Identifying hazardous or potentially hazardous situations and taking appropriate action to maintain a safe environment for self and others.
Working collaboratively with others to achieve organizational goals.
Dealing with situations and issues proactively and persistently, seizing opportunities that arise.
Listening and communicating openly, honestly, and respectfully with different audiences, promoting dialogue, and building consensus.
Treating others fairly, honestly, and respectfully, furthering the integrity of the organization and its relationships of trust with team members and the broader community.
Energizing and inspiring others to strive for excellence and commit to common goals and purposes, creating a sense of self-efficacy, resilience, and persistence in followers.
Assuming a leadership role in helping others achieve excellent results.
Recognizing the need for change, being open to new ideas and methods, and championing transformational change within the organization and beyond.

Work performed in austere field settings.

Regularly required to sit, stand, bend, reach and move about facilities.

https://apply.workable.com/billings-flyi.description: Billings Flying Service (BFS) is a solutions provider for the aviation industry, specializing in CH-47 aerial firefighting and heavy lift services. Leveraging 35+ years of experience with fixed wing and rotorcraft, BFS is positioned to solve the most difficult aviation problems. Since 1997, BFS has operated fire-fighting helicopters across the world, including Chile, Indonesia, South Korea, and the United States. The BFS fleet of CH-47Ds, UH-60A, are capable of fighting wildfires by either bucket or tank. Aircraft are supported through BFS’s FAA 145 repair station and talented staff. BFS owns and operates a FAA 145, AS9110, and Boeing certified maintenance, repair, and overhaul facility for the CH-47 Chinook. Established in 2019, the MRO facility supports the BFS Chinook fleet, commercial CH-47 customers, the United States military, and foreign militaries. The facility focuses on CH-47 drivetrain, dynamics, hydraulics, actuation, and flight controls. About the Role We are seeking an A&P Helicopter Mechanic to support our CH-47 fleet operations. You will be required to be out of town for extended periods of time, with employer paying lodging and per diem. Please call or text Bridger Blain, President at 406-697-5499. Requirements What You'll Do • Maintain a close liaison with the crew supervisor to provide current status of aircraft maintenance activity and planning for upcoming maintenance events. • Perform and document maintenance and alterations of aircraft airframe, engines, and appliances in accordance with the procedures published in the BFS MPM, approved inspection program, manufacturer’s maintenance manuals and associated service documentation, acceptable and approved data as applicable, and 14 CFR Regulations. • Ensure company aircraft are maintained in accordance with the airworthiness standards prescribed by the applicable 14 CFR regulations. • Provide after-hours maintenance on-call support for the assigned aircraft as directed. • Ensure aircraft discrepancies are accurately documented and addressed while aircraft are in revenue service and during major inspection events. • Ensure all aircraft maintenance records and associated documents to include all BFS forms and AIP checklists, equipment manufacturer’s forms and checklists, and parts approval documents are completed, processed and maintained in accordance with the BFD MPM SOP, applicable federal aviation regulations, and company policies. • Possess a thorough knowledge of Federal aviation regulations, the maintenance procedures manual, the operations manual, operations specifications and other pertinent information. • Ensure all company and personally-owned calibrated tools are within calibration time limits when used. • Maintain company-owned local Ground Support Equipment (GSE). • Perform other duties as assigned. Your Required Qualifications • Must be able to read and write English. • Must pass airport security background checks and travel for work as required. • Must have good organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities. • Must have a high school diploma or equivalent. • Must have a current and valid Driver’s License. • Must have a current and valid FAA Airframe & Powerplant License. • Frequently be required to stand, walk, continually utilize hand, and finger dexterity, climb, balance, bend, or crawl, occasionally from heights in precarious places. • Frequently lift / push / carry items up to 50 pounds. • Awareness that he / she will be working near and around moving mechanical parts, around fumes, airborne particles, or toxic chemicals and exposure to loud noises. • Ability to operate equipment and read technical information. • Exposure to weather conditions outside such as extreme heat or cold. • 5+ years working on the CH-47D or other Boeing aircraft types (preferred). • Required Qualifications: FAA A&P License, Rotorcraft experience. https://gama-aviation.my.salesforce-site.description: Due to continued growth within our MRO and Special Missions business units, we are pleased to invite candidates to apply for the position of Director of Maintenance, Special Missions. Based at either our Bournemouth or Gloucester site, with regular travel to Special Mission maintenance locations, this role will provide commercial, operational and technical leadership of our Special Missions maintenance contracts. Strategic Maintenance Planning: Own and produce short and long term rolling maintenance plans for the Special Missions fleet. Align maintenance capacity, workforce planning and resource allocation to forecast demand. Support long term planning to ensure operational resilience and customer continuity. Operational Leadership & Service Delivery: Daily oversight of Special Mission fleet activity in close collaboration with Base Maintenance Managers. Ensure aircraft availability meets or exceeds contractual SLAs. Work closely with Account Managers to protect contract performance and customer relationships for Special Missions fleet. Ensure effective engineering service delivery through close collaboration and coordination between Planning, Supply Chain, Resource and operational teams. Drive alignment between CAMO and MRO to optimise maintenance programme planning, reliability and service improvements. Identify operational risks and delivery constraints and implement corrective actions where required. Develop, promote and ensure adherence to Gama Part 145 requirements and Health & Safety policies. Provide senior level oversight to ensure daily operations remain aligned with regulatory frameworks. Commercial & Financial Performance: Own budgets across the Special Missions, including (but not limited to): Wales Air Ambulance Operation, Compass, NPAS - East Midlands, Bond Helicopters, Scottish Air Ambulance, Other Special Mission projects as applicable. Ensure financial controls are observed and operations remain within agreed budgets. Drive cost control initiatives and service improvement programmes, without compromising service delivery or compliance. Protect margin and long term contract sustainability. Provide technical and operational input into contract bids, negotiations and renewals. Skills, Qualifications and Experience required: Significant experience within Aviation Maintenance and MRO environments. Demonstrable leadership experience within a Part-145 organisation. Recognised Licensed Aircraft Engineer Licence or relevant degree (ideally within aeronautical or aircraft maintenance engineering). In-depth working knowledge of Part 145 regulations and practices. Strong leadership and people management skills. Proven ability to manage multiple stakeholders across operational and commercial functions. Strong commercial acumen with budget ownership experience. Excellent reporting and analytical capability. UK national with eligibility to obtain Security Clearance (SC). In addition to a Competitive Salary, we will offer you: Competitive Group Pension Scheme, Comprehensive Life Assurance *, Comprehensive Income Protection *, Comprehensive Travel Insurance *, Comprehensive Private Healthcare (after successful passing of probation) *, Free Car Parking, 2 Paid Volunteering Days each calendar year (subject to line manager approval), Investment in Training, Qualifications and Professional Development.

jobs.heliservice.de::33b4c2.title: Auszubildender zum Fluggerätmechaniker m/w/d Fachrichtung Instandhaltungstechnik
jobs.heliservice.de::33b4c2.description:
Mit über 35 Jahren Erfahrung im Offshore-Flugbetrieb zählt HeliService zu den Erfahrensten in der Offshore Logistik Branche. In dieser Zeit hat sich HeliService zu einem international agierenden Unternehmen entwickelt.
HeliService ist Weltmarktführer im Bereich Hoist Operation und bietet neben Intelligent Offshore Logistik und HEMS auch 3rd Party Maintenance Services an. Aufgrund ihrer weitreichenden Expertise und hohen Qualitätsstandards ist HeliService auch eines von sechs internationalen Excellence Service Center von Leonardo.
Die Wartung und Instandhaltung von HeliService führt routinemäßige Inspektionen entsprechend der Wartungspläne durch, um die Flugzeuge in optimalem Zustand zu halten. Darüber hinaus führt sie außerplanmäßige Reparaturen durch. Ein hohes Augenmerk wird auf die lückenlose Dokumentation und Qualitätskontrollen gelegt, um die gesetzlichen Standards zu erfüllen und ein Höchstmaß an Sicherheit und Lufttüchtigkeit zu gewährleisten. 
Da das Unternehmen weiter wächst, suchen wir zum 01.08.2026 wieder zwei Auszubildende zum Fluggerätmechaniker m/w/d Fachrichtung Instandhaltungstechnik. Der praktische Ausbildungsteil findet in Emden statt. Der theoretische Ausbildungsteil findet in der Berufsschule in Bremen im Blockunterricht statt. Die Fahrtkosten nach Bremen und die Unterkunft dort wird von HeliService getragen.
Unser Ziel ist es mit der Ausbildung unser Freigabeberechtigtes Personal der Zukunft von morgen zu gewinnen. Es bestehen folglich umfangreiche Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten im Unternehmen und es gibt eine Übernahmegarantie nach der Ausbildung.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::ground-operation.title: Ground Operations Officer
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::ground-operation.description: The main purpose of the Ground Operations Officer is to help and assist the pilots and act as the link between NHV Denmark and the client. Main Responsibilities Make all the preparation for flights Responsible for planning and coordination for all NHV Denmark’s flight operations Understand the client´s business and needs, under the decision and supervision of the Ground Operations Manager Liaise with customers as required/directed on delays and significant events. Assist with customer enquires or queries as required. Liaise with line internal departments and customers as required on current operational matters. Pilots scheduling Ensure all administration is carried out to the required standard and regulations Anticipate and co-ordinate required changes to the flight schedule and liaise with relevant departments. Assist the colleagues and other ad hoc work from Operation or Ground Operations Manager Responsible for complying with Information Security Management System (ISMS) policies, procedures, and controls and supporting information security objectives

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::finance-manager.title: Finance Manager
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::finance-manager.description: We are looking for a strategic and hands-on Finance Manager to lead our Group Finance function and strengthen our financial foundations for sustainable growth. Reporting directly to the CFO, the Finance Manager provides strategic and operational leadership across Finance, ensuring strong governance, regulatory compliance, commercial insight, and sustainable performance across the NHV Group. As accountable lead for Group accounting and tax compliance, you will partner closely with the CFO and senior leadership to shape international strategy, manage financial risk, and drive organizational performance. You combine strategic vision with operational excellence, continuously enhancing finance processes, systems, and policies to improve efficiency, transparency, and control. Responsibilities and areas Finance Leadership & Team Management Lead, coach, and develop a high-performing Finance team. Build a future-ready finance function aligned with business needs. Foster collaboration, accountability, and continuous professional development. Travel to other bases as and when required. Strategy, Governance & Policy Development Develop and execute the Group finance vision and strategy. Define and standardize Group accounting, tax, and transfer pricing policies. Ensure strong governance, internal controls, and regulatory compliance across all entities. Contribute to strategic planning and executive decision-making. Partner with operational teams to drive performance and growth. Financial Reporting & Consolidation Oversee accurate and timely statutory and management reporting. Lead consolidation across multiple legal entities. Ensure integrity, transparency, and compliance in all reporting processes. Act as primary contact for audits and external reviews. Deliver high-quality financial analysis, insights, scenario planning, and advice on complex accounting and risk matters. Process Improvement & Systems Optimization Drive finance transformation and efficiency initiatives. Optimise ERP systems and reporting tools. Establish scalable processes for a dynamic international environment. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and safeguard company information and assets. International Expansion & Compliance Lead finance setup for new legal entities in collaboration with external advisors. Ensure compliance with international tax frameworks and transfer pricing regulations. Budget & Operational Management Manage departmental budget and resource allocation. Deliver finance outputs on time and to high quality standards. Travel to operational bases as required.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::aw-139-co-pilot.title: AW139 Co-Pilot
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::aw-139-co-pilot.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Co-Pilot ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base. Your Role As an AW139 Co-Pilot, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Assisting the pilot-in-command with administrative flight duties Understanding the client’s business & needs, under the decision and supervision of the Chief Pilot Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Code of Conduct, Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Support the pilot-in-command in maintaining a proper standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Act responsible to the pilot-in-command in assisting to conduct an efficient flight Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::technical-direct.title: Technical Director
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::technical-direct.description: We are seeking an experienced and strategic Technical Director to lead and oversee all technical helicopter operations across our multi-base organization. Reporting directly to the CEO and serving on the Extended Executive Committee, you will hold full accountability for Maintenance (Part-145), CAMO, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, and Part-147 training. You will ensure safe, compliant, efficient, and customer-focused technical operations while driving performance, cost control, and fleet availability. This is a high-impact executive role combining operational leadership with strong business acumen and full P&L responsibility. You will lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, and sustainability programs, positioning the organization for future growth. ⚠️ This function can be performed from any of the NHV bases. Regular travel to other bases is required. Responsibilities and areas Operational & Strategic Leadership Lead and optimize all technical functions (Part-145 Maintenance, CAMO, Engineering, Supply Chain, Logistics, Fleet Management, Part-147) with a strong business-driven and performance-focused approach. Develop and execute technical strategies, structures, and processes aligned with corporate objectives, ensuring efficiency, cost-effectiveness, and operational excellence. Drive organizational optimization and continuous improvement initiatives across maintenance and supply chain operations. Ensure technical capabilities fully support operational delivery, customer commitments, and strategic growth objectives. Establish, monitor, and report on KPIs covering safety, compliance, service quality, cost efficiency, and fleet availability. Ensure compliance with ISMS policies and execute additional executive-level technical assignments as required. Financial & Performance Management Hold full P&L responsibility for technical operations, including maintenance budgets, supply chain expenditures, and lifecycle cost management. Drive sustainable cost optimization and total cost of ownership improvements across the fleet. Partner with Finance to ensure disciplined budgeting, forecasting, financial oversight, and structured performance reporting. Balance profitability objectives with uncompromising safety and regulatory compliance standards. People Leadership & Culture Provide strategic leadership to multi-location technical teams, building strong leadership capability, succession planning, and skills development. Establish clear performance goals, accountability frameworks, and KPI-driven management structures. Foster a culture aligned with company values, embedding safety, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Promote strong cross-functional alignment and transparent executive communication. Safety, Compliance & Governance Ensure full compliance with AOC, (B)CAA, EASA/CAA/ICAO regulations, and internal standards across all technical domains. Oversee governance of Accountable Managers and Postholders (Part-145, CAMO, Supply Chain, Fleet Management, Part-147). Maintain robust operational controls, reporting systems, documentation, approvals, and regulatory records. Champion airworthiness, safety, quality assurance, and consistent adherence to procedures and best practices. Transformation, Innovation & Continuous Improvement Lead technical transformation initiatives including fleet optimization, digitalization, predictive maintenance, sustainability programs, and SAF adoption. Introduce innovative technologies and structured change management to enhance reliability, safety, availability, and cost efficiency. Drive continuous performance improvement across all technical and logistical functions. Stakeholder Management Build and maintain strong relationships with OEMs, suppliers, MROs, regulators, aviation authorities, and customers. Represent the technical organization at executive and regulatory levels. Ensure external partnerships and contractual commitments align with internal technical strategy and support business growth.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::flight-operation.title: Flight Operations Officer
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::flight-operation.description: The Flight Operations Officer is responsible for the execution of the daily base flight schedule for our base in Den Helder. They will interface with a number of internal and external stakeholders to manage the safe, compliant and efficient delivery of the program, whilst upholding a high standard of service to NHV’s clients. The position is an operational as well as administrative function, part of the operations department, and reports directly to the Service Delivery Lead. This is a part-time function (20 hours / week). Depending on the candidate and preferences, this could be extended. Responsibilities and areas Flight Planning & Coordination Prepare and maintain daily flight schedules, including flight watch and monitoring. Anticipate and coordinate required changes to the flight schedule. Provide operational paperwork and briefings to flight crews. Monitor weather and NOTAMs relevant to flight operations. Crew Management Manage pilot scheduling and ensure compliance with Flight Time Limitations (FTL). Liaise with crew members regarding operational updates and changes.  Client & Stakeholder Liaison Act as a key point of contact for clients regarding flight changes, disruptions, and queries. Liaise with Air Traffic Control and airport authorities as needed. Communicate with internal departments (Engineering, Ground Operations, etc.) to ensure fleet availability and operational readiness.  Administration & Compliance Ensure all operational and customer reports are completed accurately and on time. Maintain high standards of documentation in line with regulatory and company requirements.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::aw-139-captain.title: AW139 Captain
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::aw-139-captain.description: Are you an experienced AW139 Captain ready to operate in a professional offshore environment? At our Den Helder base, you will perform scheduled shuttle flights supporting offshore Oil & Gas operations, ensuring the highest standards of safety, efficiency and customer service. You will operate in a highly structured and safety-driven environment where professionalism and teamwork are essential. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the Den Helder base. Your Role As an AW139 Captain, you are responsible for executing flights in the safest and most cost-effective manner, in full compliance with aviation regulations, company procedures and operational standards. You report to the Chief Pilot and play a key role in representing the company towards our offshore clients. What You’ll Be Doing Flight Preparation & Execution Perform full technical preparation of flights (route planning, fuel calculations, aircraft inspection) Execute flights in accordance with SOPs, limitations and performance requirements Ensure all aircraft safety systems are fully operational Complete accurate pre- and post-flight administration Report any incidents, defects or irregularities Compliance & Professional Standards Maintain full familiarity with national and international aviation legislation Operate in accordance with the Operations Manual, FOM, FOIs and company procedures Respect company policies including Safety, Quality and Drugs & Alcohol policies Maintain high standards of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance Offshore & Team Responsibilities Support offshore Oil & Gas operations as assigned by the Chief Pilot Remain flexible for operational needs Demonstrate strong customer service awareness Lead by example and promote team spirit Adapt to local culture, environment and client needs

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::experienced-hho-.title: Experienced HHO Operator – Licensed Engineer
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::experienced-hho-.description: The helicopter Hoist Operator plays a critical role combining technical skills as a Licensed Line Engineer working on the helicopter and performing hoist flights hoisting passengers to the wind farms. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Helicopter Hoist operator (HHO) Carry out HHO flight duties, in accordance with NHV Operations SOP's. Know the NHV procedures and the Flight Operations Manual. Inspect hoist systems, cables, hooks, harnesses, and safety equipment. Conduct safety briefings with passengers and flight crew. Coordinate passenger- and aircraft handling procedures before and after flights. Assist with the technical preparation for the flight(s). Operate hoist controls smoothly to raise/lower personnel or cargo. Provide precise directional guidance to the pilot (clearance, obstacles, positioning). Immediately halt operations if unsafe conditions arise. Use standardized aviation terminology. Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as respecting the Drugs & Alcohol policy. Line Maintenance Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. Ensure all safety regulations are followed Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::b-1-3-engineer-a.title: B1.3 Engineer AW139
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::b-1-3-engineer-a.description: Are you an experienced EASA Part 66 B1.3 Licensed Engineer ready to work in a dynamic offshore helicopter environment? At our Den Helder base, you will play a key role in keeping our AW139 fleet mission-ready and safe. You’ll join a close-knit team of 8 engineers, working in a professional yet supportive environment where safety, teamwork and technical excellence come first. Please note: Due to operational requirements, candidates must reside within reasonable commuting distance of the base. Your Role As a B1.3 Engineer, you are responsible for performing and certifying line maintenance in accordance with approved maintenance data and company procedures. You work closely with fellow engineers under the supervision of the Shift Supervisor, reporting hierarchically to the Base Manager and functionally to the Maintenance Lead. You are more than a certifying engineer — you are a problem solver, safety ambassador and key contributor to operational continuity. What You’ll Be Doing Line Maintenance & Technical Excellence Perform and certify maintenance tasks in accordance with approved maintenance data Execute work orders while respecting all applicable restrictions Raise non-routine tasks and defect entries for unscheduled findings Ensure Independent Inspections on safety-critical tasks Record and certify all maintenance accurately in the work file Safety & Compliance Follow all safety regulations and MOE procedures Report safety hazards and occurrences via the reporting system Flag inaccurate, incomplete or unclear procedures Maintain a clean and orderly workspace with properly maintained tools Operational Support Contribute to flexible and efficient work planning Ensure maintenance is completed within operational deadlines Coordinate with Supply Chain, Stores and support services to guarantee parts availability Perform daily technical analysis and problem-solving based on crew feedback

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::b-1-3-and-b-2-en.title: B1.3 and B2 H175 Engineer
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::b-1-3-and-b-2-en.description: The helicopter B1.3 or B2 Engineer plays a critical role ensuring the helicopter is ready and fit for flight and is in an airworthy condition.  Performing all required scheduled and unscheduled line maintenance. Safety management and good communication play a crucial role. You work in a small team of engineers, pilots and hoist operators following the helicopter to different operational locations for crew changes and hoisting on wind farms. You report functionally to the Maintenance Lead who oversees the maintenance and hierarchically to the Base Manager in Esbjerg. You will follow the aircraft in bases in Denmark, the Netherlands, Germany, Poland, Belgium or any other European country the customer requests. Responsibilities and Main Results Areas Line Maintenance ·         Ensure that all maintenance tasks carried out to helicopters meet the standard of the BCAA/EASA and MOE instructions ·         Work on aircraft and aircraft components as per Work Order, whilst respecting any restriction applicable. ·         Ensure all safety regulations are followed ·         Report any safety hazard and mandatory reportable occurrence via the NHV occurrence reporting system. ·         Ensure correct following and performing of all maintenance instructions as detailed in the approved maintenance data and ensure correct following of all MOE procedures. ·         Report if any procedure is inaccurate, incomplete or ambiguous. ·         Record all performed maintenance in the work file and ensure all tasks are properly certified. ·         Raise non-routine tasks or defect entries in the system for all unscheduled findings. ·         Ensure Independent Inspections are performed on all safety critical tasks. ·         Keep the workspace clean and tidy and keep the tools and equipment in good working order. ·         Ensure, together with all engineers, a variable & flexible work planning & work roster based on business continuity. ·         Ensure maintenance is completed before due and within the appropriate time limits to protect the operational needs. ·         Ensure, in cooperation with NHV Supply Chain and support services that the necessary maintenance material & spare parts are present on site at the planned moment. ·         Ensure, based on pilot & other relevant information, daily problem analysis & problem solving. Hoist Operations ·         A combination of an Engineering position and a Helicopter Hoist Operator (HHO) position is a possibility but not a requirement. Refer to the job description HHO for more details.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::h-175-co-pilot.title: H175 Co-Pilot
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::h-175-co-pilot.description: To execute scheduled offshore energy flights in the safest and most efficient manner in accordance with aviation regulation and whilst upholding NHV standards. Responsibilities Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel. Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Ops Manuals, Notices To Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's business & needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot. Performing other assigned flying duties as required Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures. Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance. Completing tasks allocated by the Chief Pilot as required Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs & Alcohol policy.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::mechanic-aberdee.title: Mechanic - Aberdeen
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::mechanic-aberdee.description: NHV Group is an international helicopter service provider, specialised in business-to-business helicopter services both offshore & onshore. We aim to create a leading position in the helicopter world with a primary focus on Europe and Africa, without compromising our safety. We strive to provide an increasing level of flexibility to our customers. Our services and experience present a refreshing and safe alternative in the industry. NHV cares about it’s people and invests in them to shape the best professionals in the industry. At NHV, we are dedicated to delivering excellence in the helicopter world. With a commitment to safety, innovation, and customer centricity, we provide reliable services that support a wide range of missions. nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::mechanic.title: Mechanic
nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::mechanic.description: As a Mechanic in the base maintenance hangar of Ostend, you will be responsible for performing heavy base maintenance on Airbus H175 and H145 helicopters. You will work as part of the base maintenance team under coordination of the Dock Chief and in collaboration with Licensed Engineers. With the right experience and progression, this role offers the opportunity to evolve into a Licensed Engineer position. Responsibilities & Main Result Areas Maintenance tasks on aircraft and components in accordance with issued work orders, applicable manuals, and approved maintenance data. Carry out activities such as: Disassembly & reassembly Major inspections (H175 heavy checks, H145 scheduled inspections) Rectify defects Component replacements and system adjustments Ensure all safety regulations, PPE requirements, and hangar procedures are followed at all times. Immediately report safety hazards or mandatory occurrences via the NHV occurrence reporting system. Follow all MOE procedures and notify the Dock Chief if instructions or processes are unclear, incomplete, or ambiguous. Record all performed maintenance accurately and clearly in the work file. Raise defect entries or non-routine tasks for any findings discovered during inspections. Support Licensed Engineers during troubleshooting and functional checks. Maintain a clean, safe, and organized working environment, including proper care of tools and ground equipment. nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::co-pilot.description: Responsibilities and Main Result Areas ·         Assisting the Pilot-in-Command with the technical preparation of flights including, but not limited to, route planning, fuel calculation, performance limitations and general inspection of the helicopter ·         Ensuring that all pre and post flight administration is completed accurately under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command ·         Assisting the Pilot-in-Command as required with the raising of any reportable incidents and issues post flight via the correct channel. ·         Conducting pilot duties under the supervision of the Pilot-in-Command whilst fully complying with all safety rules, requirements, limitations and SOPs with full regard to the helicopter type and scope of flight operation ·         Working alongside the Pilot-in-Command to ensure that all safety systems and equipment are functioning correctly ·         Maintaining a good working knowledge of NHV Operations Manuals, Notices to Pilots, other related manuals, instructions and procedures ·         Consistently demonstrating a good awareness of customer service requirements and understanding the client's needs, under the supervision of the Chief Pilot. ·         Performing other assigned flying duties as required ·         Maintaining familiarity with all relevant national and international air legislation, agreed aviation practices and local procedures. ·         Maintaining the correct standard of crew discipline, conduct and personal appearance. ·         Completing tasks allocated by the Flight Operations Department as required ·         Leading by example and promoting a team spirit amongst colleagues. ·         Respecting the code of conduct and overall behaviour towards the whole NHV team, as well as adhering to the Drugs and Alcohol policy.

nhv-group.jobtoolz.com::executive-assist.title: Executive Assistant

castleair.co.uk/careers/deputy-safety-an.title: Senior Supply Chain Manager
castleair.co.uk/careers/deputy-safety-an.description:
Castle Air Aviation Group is seeking a highly experienced Senior Supply Chain Manager to lead and manage all procurement, stores and logistics functions across Castle Air UK and overseas bases, ensuring full regulatory compliance, cost-effective procurement, optimal stock availability, and strong operational support to maintenance and flight operations.
About Castle Air:
Castle Air has been a leader in rotary aviation for over 40 years, offering engineering, charter, and training services across the UK and Europe. We pride ourselves on our commitment to safety, innovation, and excellence.
Key Responsibilities:
• Lead, manage, and develop all Stores and Supply Chain staff, fostering teamwork and high performance.
• Define departmental objectives, KPIs, and performance standards.
• Ensure adequate resourcing, training (Aerotrac, regulatory), succession planning, and competency management.
• Act as primary escalation point for supply chain issues (AOG, vendor failures, logistics delays).
• Oversee all purchasing activities: parts, consumables, tooling, GSE, and contracted services.
• Manage supplier selection, negotiation, contract management, and performance evaluation.
• Develop procurement strategies to reduce cost, improve lead times, and manage risks.
• Support aircraft teardown, part-out, storage, preservation, and part-sales processes.
• Maintain strong relationships with OEMs, distributors, repair vendors, and partners.
• Ensure all stores are fully Part 145 compliant, including storage conditions, segregation, and traceability.
• Oversee tooling calibration programmes and compliance monitoring.
• Develop stock level forecasting models and monitor stock accuracy, obsolescence, and rotable turnaround.
• Ensure efficient import/export processes, customs compliance, and Dangerous Goods handling.
• Lead AOG logistics support and oversee shipping, distribution, and dispatch standards.
• Optimise use of Aerotrac (or other ERP/MRP) systems for inventory and logistics.
• Drive continuous improvement initiatives and support ISO 9001:2015 compliance.
• Maintain compliance with UK CAA/EASA Part 145, Part M, ADR/IATA DG, export control, and customs regulations.
• Support audits, implement corrective actions, and improve procedures.
• Act as key contact for supply chain matters with internal and external stakeholders.
• Provide oversight for basic facilities support and coordinate third-party maintenance services.
What You’ll Bring:
• Proven experience in aviation supply chain and stores management.
• Strong leadership and team development skills.
• Excellent knowledge of regulatory compliance (UK CAA/EASA Part 145, Part M).
• Skilled in procurement, vendor management, and contract negotiation.
• Proficient in ERP/MRP systems (Aerotrac preferred).
• Strong analytical, organisational, and communication skills.
Requirements added by the job poster
• Eligibility to work in UK
• 8+ years of experience in Supply Chain

Who Are We:
Life Link III is a premier, nationally recognized air medical transport company, known for our clinical excellence, superior aviation program and reliable response in providing care to critically ill or injured patients. Our priority is to do everything possible to give every patient their best chance for the best possible outcome - demonstrated by our unwavering commitment to providing the highest level of care to our patients while on board safe, state-of-the-art aircraft.  We continually focus on innovation and work to put cutting-edge technology and education in the hands of our flight medical crew, pilots, mechanics, operational control specialists and communication specialists so we can provide uncompromising care to the communities we serve.  We do this work while living out our core values of safety, customer focus, excellence, integrity, innovation, and collaboration.
As a Rotor Wing Pilot at Life Link III, you’ll be part of an elite air medical team, flying state-of-the-art aircraft to transport medical crews and patients to the care they need. You’ll play a vital role in every mission, ensuring safe and professional flights while navigating complex aviation regulations and company procedures. In this position, you’ll combine your expert piloting skills with a commitment to safety, teamwork, and making a real difference for patients and their families.
Your Impact
:
Responsible for safety of passengers and crew, and oversees all flights accepted
Effectively communicate and collaborate with flight crew, dispatch and partners
Actively shares flight knowledge with team members and serves as a resource to discuss transport, flight operations, and other work-related topics
Follows all regulations, manuals, and company policies during flights
Completes flight documentation accurately and promptly
Supervises loading of aircraft, refueling and ensures safe weight and balance
Adheres to proper Crew Resources Management (CRM) technique
Actively participates in the Life Link III Safety Management System
Responsible for pertinent information regarding all flights, including NOTAMs, TFRs, and weather information
Work cohesively as a unit to accomplish all Life Link III assignments including patient transfers
Responsible for maintaining qualifications as required in CFRs
Proactively pursues professional growth and supports the learning and development of others

Commercial pilot certificate and instrument rating for category and class of aircraft
Current FAA Class I or II Medical Certificate
2000 total flight hours or meets CAMTS equivalency
1500 hours Helicopter flight hours
1000 hours PIC in category
500 hours of rotorcraft turbine time
200 hours of cross country
50 hours of unaided night can be accepted with a minimum of 100 hours of aided night
50 hours of hood or actual time instrument time in flight in category.  (Simulator time does not count.) A pilot that is dual qualified in fixed wing with 100 hours of actual instrument time can reduce this requirement to 25 hours.
Able to work cooperatively and collaboratively with all

Prior experience in HAA operations
Prior Search and Rescue Experience
ATP Certificate
Bachelor’s degree

Helicopter Captain / First Officer (Air Ambulance Operations)
jobs.smartrecruiters.com/CanadianHelicop.description: Join our elite crew and embark on an exciting career path within our industry. As one of top leaders in the industry, we have an exciting opportunity for a Captain and First Officer on the S76, in support of our Air Ambulance Operations in Halifax, Nova Scotia. If you are looking to further your career, we invite you to apply today and be a part of our growing team! Responsibilities Captain : Act as a Captain as per Company operation standards; Perform IFR Flights in challenging and rapidly changing environment; Be able to work closely with our customer, respond efficiently to aircraft tasking, while maintaining a high level of safety; Coordination and effective communication between crew and dispatch All duties assigned by the Chief Pilot or Base Manager; First Officer: Act as a pilot as per Company operation standards; Any other responsibility related to the position Qualifications Common requirements Must be willing to relocate to Halifax, Nova Scotia. A valid Canadian passport or a valid authorization to work in Canada and to travel to USA, (this position is not available for sponsorship); Valid Class 1 Medical; Ability to successfully complete the Controlled Goods Security assessment application for NVG purposes; Immunization records with required vaccinations are mandatory. Be customer focused and a team player; Have good communication skills and a positive attitude; A Criminal record search will be conducted prior to hire. Requirements specific by position Captain: A valid and unrestricted Transport Canada ATPL(H) License; A minimum of 2000 hours rotary wing flight time; A minimum of 500 hours as Pilot-in-Command (PIC) which 200 hours of Multi-Engine Aircraft; Minimum of 2 years of experience in Instrument Flight Operations with actual IFR flight hours as PIC; Previous experience flying in a 703 Air Ambulance operation as a Captain is preferred; NVG experience an asset; Endorsement on S76 considered an asset; First Officer: Have a valid ATPL or have passed ATPL written exams within the previous 12 months; Hold a valid Commercial Rotary-wing License; Have 500 hours Rotary-wing time; Transport Canada Group IV Instrument rating; Possess unrestricted Night Rating; Possess previous Night / IFR flying experience in the Maritime environment is an asset. Additional Information Remuneration: Competitive salary according to experience. Schedule : Shifts schedule. Not a touring position as you will be based out of Halifax, NS. Working for Canadian Helicopters is: A unique occasion to grow professionally in a dynamic environment full of opportunities. Working with passionate, committed people who enjoy surpassing themselves. Being part of an organizational culture based on fundamental principles. Having an employer of choice offering competitive working conditions. We offer a flexible benefits program including comprehensive medical and dental coverage, life insurance, short and long term disability insurance and a pension plan, depending on employment status. Canadian Helicopters Limited is committed to employment equity and diversity and encourages applications from women, Indigenous people, and visible minorities. For more information we invite you to visit our website: www.canadianhelicopters.com Follow us on LinkedIn: https://ca.linkedin.com/company/canadian-helicopters-limited

jobs.smartrecruiters.com/CanadianHelicop.description:
The Aeronautical Components Technician reports directly to the Engine and Components Overhaul Shop Manager. He is responsible for the inspection, repair or overhaul of aeronautical components. He ensures that the execution of his tasks is carried out in accordance with the airworthiness standards established by the manufacturer, the Canadian Aviation Regulations as well as those of the company.

Keep personal qualifications up to date;

Analytical mind, problem-solving skills and organizational skills.

Technicien d’entretien d’aéronefs (Plusieurs emplacements)
jobs.smartrecruiters.com/CanadianHelicop.description:
Technicien d’entretien d’aéronefs (Plusieurs emplacements)
Temps plein
Location de la base: Autre
Description de l'entreprise
Hélicoptères Canadiens est une entreprise de services de transport par hélicoptère, de soutien à la maintenance et de formation au pilotage. En affaires depuis 1947, Hélicoptères Canadiens est le plus grand exploitant d'hélicoptères au Canada avec plus de 20 emplacements à travers le pays et une flotte de près de 100 appareils monomoteurs et multimoteurs.
Description du poste
Les tâches du TEA comprennent:
Effectuer l’entretien des aéronefs et la planification;
S’assurer que toutes les pièces de rechange sont inspectées et correctement certifiées avant l’installation;
Participer à l’organisation et à la bonne tenue du hangar;
Assurer le respect de toutes les politiques et procédures de l’entreprise;
Se déplacer vers divers lieux de travail et environnements au besoin;
Effectuer des tâches connexes assignées par le superviseur.
Plusieurs sites disponibles:
Southport (MB)
Pool Across Canada
Qualifications
Détenir une licence de Transport Canada M1 et/ou M2 valide;
Détenir le droit légal de travailler au Canada, ce poste n'est pas disponible pour un parrainage;
Détenir, avoir détenu ou être admissible à détenir une autorisation certifiée en aéronefs (ACA) sur un ou plusieurs des appareils suivants :
AS350B2
AS350B3
AS355N
Série B206
B212
B412
S61
note: détenir une ACA sur d'autres types d'avions CHL serait un atout certain;
Sens de l’organisation et initiative;
Orientation service client, bonne aptitude au niveau de la communication, attitude positive et esprit d'équipe;
Disponible pour faire des tours à l’extérieur de la base selon les besoins du client.
Capacité à naviguer et à travailler efficacement dans un environnement Windows.
Il peut être nécessaire d’obtenir une autorisation d’accès aux marchandises contrôlées avec l’approbation de l’entreprise.
Selon le lieu d'affectation, des qualifications supplémentaires pourraient être requises, telles que :
Avoir réussi un cours de maintenance de base dispensé par un organisme de formation agréé par Transports Canada, Aviation civile (TCAC) ou par le MDN;
- Southport (MB)
Les candidats possédant une qualification en maintenance d’aéronefs militaires canadiens seront considérés. - Southport (MB)
Informations supplémentaires
Rémuneration:
Salaire compétitif selon expérience.
Travailler pour Hélicoptères Canadiens, c’est :
Une opportunité unique d'évoluer professionnellement dans un environnement dynamique et plein d'opportunités ;
Travailler avec des personnes passionnées, engagées et qui aiment se dépasser ;
Faire partie d'une culture organisationnelle basée sur des principes fondamentaux;
Avoir un employeur de choix offrant des conditions de travail compétitives.
Nous offrons un programme flexible d'avantages sociaux incluant une protection médicale et dentaire complète, une assurance vie, une assurance invalidité de courte et de longue durée et un régime de fonds de pension, selon le statut d'emploi.
Hélicoptères Canadiens Limitée souscrit à l’équité en matière d’emploi et la diversité et encourage les candidatures venant des femmes, des Autochtones, des personnes handicapées et des minorités visibles.
Pour de plus amples informations, nous vous invitons à visiter notre site web à l’adresse
https://www.canadianhelicopters.com/fr/

That spirit of innovation and excellence drives everything we do – from our state-of-the-art emergency medical services program, to our new and expanding graduate medical education programs, to our progressive care models that focus on preventive care. These are just some of the ways we go above and beyond to help the communities we serve.
To stay up to date on all things Geisinger Emergency Medicine, connect with us on LinkedIn
Our Teams
Advanced Practice Providers
A vital part of our care team, Geisinger’s Emergency Medicine APPs can expect the best development resources and training to thrive in their role. Geisinger’s Department of Advanced Practice Providers serves to support our Advanced Practitioners and Advanced Practitioner students through interdepartmental consultation, benchmarking of best practices, professional practice standards, compensation, personnel management, and care delivery models. In addition to a comprehensive range of benefits, candidates also receive a $20k recruitment incentive to join our team.
Pediatric Emergency Medicine
Our team is bringing top-notch Pediatric Emergency Medicine to rural communities across central Pennsylvania through an innovative approach to pediatric readiness. Our regional strategy leverages Pediatric Emergency Medicine specialist knowledge and skills to support our outstanding Emergency Medicine colleagues in caring for children across our 10-hospital system. We use telemedicine, clinical guideline development with electronic health record integration, pediatric equipment standardization, clinical tool development, education and simulation initiatives, participation in national collaboratives, local and regional quality improvement, an amazing system Pediatric Emergency Care Coordinator, and a regional, multidisciplinary Pediatric Emergency Care Coordinator (PECC) council to help deliver high quality care at diverse emergency departments across a large geographic distribution.
Central to the coordination of pediatric emergency care within our system is the colorful, 9-bed “Peds Zone” in the Emergency Department at the Janet Weis Children’s Hospital at Geisinger Medical Center (Pediatric Emergency Room | Geisinger) in Danville, PA. The “Peds Zone” will be expanding to a 14-bed unit in the next few years with the completion of a critical care tower expansion project (GMC Expansion | Geisinger | News Release). The Janet Weis Children’s Hospital (Janet Weis Children’s Hospital & CHOP | Geisinger) at Geisinger Medical Center is a 90-bed children’s hospital with a 14-bed PICU and 41-bed NICU that provides a comprehensive range of pediatric services including a full array of medical and surgical pediatric subspecialties. We are also establishing a second hub for Pediatric Emergency Medicine at the Geisinger Wyoming Valley Hospital Emergency Department in Wilkes-Barre, PA.
In addition to providing outstanding bedside care and coordinating pediatric emergency care throughout our system, our team is very involved in resident education, national collaboratives, and research. We work closely as faculty to teach and mentor Pediatrics, Med/Peds, Emergency Medicine Residents, and medical students through the Geisinger Commonwealth Medical School (Geisinger College of Health Sciences).
Recent Geisinger PEM faculty accomplishments include: EMSC Scholars appointment (Meet the 2022 EMSC Scholars • EIIC)
EMSC Fellowship appointment (Steve "Jason" Crellin, DO • EIIC)
2024 Amanda Wertz Memorial Emergency Medical Services for Children Award (Geisinger pediatric emergency medicine physician receives award)
Pediatric Sepsis Emergency Criteria Expert Panel Membership Geisinger Academy of Educators Distinguished membership (Academy of Educators | Geisinger College of Health Sciences)
GCSOM Medical Student Summer Research Immersion Program mentorship (Summer Research Immersion Program | Geisinger Commonwealth)
Academic presentations at Pediatric Academic Society, Pennsylvania ACEP, The American Medical Informatics Association
Journal Publications through the American Journal of Emergency Medicine, Pediatrics
Textbook chapter authorship in Fleischer Ludwig and Pediatric Critical Care Study Guide
Ultrasound
Collaboration and knowledge sharing are top-of-mind here. We offer system-wide clinical ultrasound administration opportunities, where everyone can collaborate across departments. There’s also a monthly multidisciplinary image review conference with different departments like Critical Care, Cardiology, and Sports Medicine. Our teams conduct weekly QA of images obtained in the emergency department, used to develop ultrasound quality improvement projects. Other perks include a one-year Accredited Emergency Ultrasound Fellowship within the Emergency Ultrasound Fellowship Accreditation Council, based at GMC (Danville, PA), as well as archiving and quality assurance services.
Research
Our research team works closely with the EM residency program leadership to improve resident training and foster healthy intellectual dialogue. In 2023, our Emergency Department published 21 peer-reviewed articles and three book chapters/textbooks and presented our work at 70 scientific conferences. System-wide, we had >$57 million in external grant and contract revenue, >1,400 active research projects, including clinical trials, and >1,090 publications.
EMS
We’re proud of our robust emergency medical services program that includes nine state-of-the-art Geisinger Life Flight® helicopters stationed at six bases and two Critical Care Ground Transports.
Education
At Geisinger, ‘learning’ is one of our core values. Fortunately, our environment is uniquely suited, with diverse pathologies, patient populations, and acuities, to educate the best and brightest minds in medicine, including Geisinger Commonwealth School of Medicine students and Geisinger Emergency Medicine residents. We are excited to expand our graduate medical education of EM residents with our second EM residency program based at Geisinger Wyoming Valley in Wilkes-Barre, PA (coming in 2026). Our first program, established in 1976 at Geisinger Medical Center in Danville, PA, is one of the oldest of its kind and maintains full accreditation from the ACGME Residency Review Committee.
System Triage Officers
When our ED patients need expedited transfers within the health system, we rely on our System Triage Officers (STOs) to help make it happen. Geisinger’s STOs are Emergency Medicine physicians who work virtually to ensure proper patient movement throughout the health system.
Trauma
Our trauma centers provide immediate care for patients with severe or life-threatening injuries. Geisinger Medical Center and Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center are Level 1 trauma centers, equipped to provide the highest level of trauma care to those who are critically ill or injured. To learn more about Geisinger’s extensive trauma services, click here.
Emergency medicine physicians at Geisinger have significantly broadened their influence both within and beyond the emergency department by serving in other key leadership and strategic roles, including Hospital Chief Medical Officers, Utilization Management Review, Information Technology, Population Health, Employee Health, Geisinger Health Plan, and various Hospital and System Quality Committees.

Well-being
As Emergency Medicine professionals, stress, emotional fatigue, grief, and burnout are part of the complex reality we live in. At Geisinger, we believe it's crucial to prioritize your own well-being to care for others. That’s why we created the Center for Well- Being, whose mission is to foster a culture that supports our teams, both professionally and personally. These services include 1:1 well-being consultation, peer support, group well-being workshops, Cabana virtual support, and well-being navigators.

jobs.smartrecruiters.com/CanadianHelicop.title: Coordonnateur(trice) du département du matériel
jobs.smartrecruiters.com/CanadianHelicop.description:
Cette personne relèvera directement du gestionnaire du matériel et sera responsable de diverses fonctions administratives du département du matériel.
Les tâches spécifiques comprennent, sans s'y limiter :
Aider à l'inventaire et mettre à jour les données du système en fonction des dénombrements réels.
Doit émettre/recevoir des pièces vers/de l'aéronef en utilisant les formulaires de base et les livres techniques des appareils.
Doit créer et mettre à jour les données des fournisseurs dans Airsoft.
Qualifications
Doit détenir l'autorisation de travailler au Canada (ce poste n'est pas disponible pour le parrainage).
Ce poste nécessite l'accès à des marchandises contrôlées ; par conséquent, le candidat retenu doit être admissible au Programme des marchandises contrôlées.
Excellentes capacités d'organisation et de communication.
Capacité à gérer et à s'adapter à l'évolution des priorités.
Bonnes compétences en informatique (La connaissance de l'Airsoft est un atout).
Capacité à travailler dans un environnement de groupe.
Bonnes compétences en communication en anglais et en français (En tant qu'entreprise pancanadienne, le bilinguisme est nécessaire pour assurer la communication entre toutes les bases à travers le pays).
Informations supplémentaires
Statut :
Permanent, temps plein
Lieu :
Les Cèdres, Qc. 100% présentiel
Horaire :
40 heures par semaine, lundi au vendredi
Travailler pour Hélicoptères Canadiens, c'est :
Une opportunité unique d'évoluer professionnellement dans un environnement dynamique et rempli d'opportunités ;
Travailler avec des personnes passionnées, engagées et qui aiment se dépasser;
Faire partie d'une culture organisationnelle basée sur des principes fondamentaux;
Avoir un employeur de choix offrant des conditions de travail compétitives.
Nous offrons un programme d'avantages sociaux flexible comprenant une couverture médicale et dentaire complète, une assurance vie, une assurance invalidité de courte et longue durée et un régime de fonds de pension.
Hélicoptères Canadiens Limitée s'engage envers l'équité en matière d'emploi et la diversité et encourage les candidatures des femmes, des Autochtones, personnes handicapées, et des minorités visibles.
Pour plus d'information, nous vous invitons à visiter notre site Web :
www.canadianhelicopters.com
